mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and income expansion, which drives consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2012 payroll level 132,821k indicates ongoing post-recession hiring momentum
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive employment trend
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS momentum signal falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months do not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near zero (below 0.25%) signals extreme monetary accommodation that suppresses discount rates and encourages equity risk-taking, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as liquidity flows into equities.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2012 FEDFUNDS at 0.13% confirms zero lower bound regime
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return <= 0 or t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) signals strengthening aggregate demand and consumer confidence. The joint signal of demand expansion across two independent channels produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2012 HOUST at 698k and RSAFS at 407.8B show concurrent demand strength
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion across consumer and housing sectors
failure_condition: Joint signal (both RSAFS and HOUST trending up) produces 20-day returns not significantly different from baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1% month-over-month signals manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side expansion, which supports corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2012 INDPRO at 96.21 reflects post-recession industrial recovery
regime_hint: supply-side expansion and capacity utilization recovery
failure_condition: Months with INDPRO growth > 1.0% do not produce significantly higher 60-day forward returns than months with lower growth (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 8.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2012 UNRATE at 8.2% indicates elevated joblessness persisting post-crisis
regime_hint: elevated unemployment regime with weak labor demand
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 8.0%, 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (mean return >= 0 or t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI growth accelerating above 2.5% annually signals monetary policy constraints and potential future rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as discount rates rise and real returns compress.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2012 CPIAUCSL at 229.10 shows moderate inflation pressure
regime_hint: inflation acceleration regime with monetary policy tightening risk
failure_condition: Periods with 12-month CPI growth > 2.5% do not produce significantly lower 60-day forward returns than lower inflation periods (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating below 2.0% annualized signals weakening aggregate demand and economic slack, which reduces corporate earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Q3 2011 GDPC1 at 13,429B reflects post-crisis recovery plateau
regime_hint: sub-trend growth regime with demand weakness
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized do not produce significantly lower 60-day forward returns than higher growth quarters (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining more than 10% month-over-month combined with retail sales (RSAFS) declining signals synchronized demand contraction across housing and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST monthly decline > 10% AND RSAFS monthly decline > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:February 2012 HOUST at 698k and RSAFS at 407.8B provide baseline for demand shock detection
regime_hint: synchronized demand contraction across housing and consumer sectors
failure_condition: Months meeting both decline triggers do not produce significantly lower 20-day forward returns than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC of joint signal < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
